(opens new window) (opens new window) (opens new window) (opens new window) (opens new window) (opens new window)

# TSLint

⚠️ TSLint is deprecated.

See this issue for more details: Roadmap: TSLint → ESLint (opens new window). If you're interested in helping with the TSLint/ESLint migration, please check out our OSS Fellowship (opens new window) program.

TSLint is an extensible static analysis tool that checks TypeScript (opens new window) code for readability, maintainability, and functionality errors. It is widely supported across modern editors & build systems and can be customized with your own lint rules, configurations, and formatters.

TSLint currently supports:

  • an extensive set of core rules
  • custom lint rules
  • custom formatters (failure reporters)
  • inline disabling and enabling of rules with comment flags in source code
  • configuration presets (tslint:latest, tslint-react, etc.) and plugin composition
  • automatic fixing of formatting & style violations
  • integration with MSBuild (opens new window), Grunt (opens new window), Gulp (opens new window), Atom (opens new window), Eclipse (opens new window), Emacs (opens new window), Sublime (opens new window), Vim (opens new window), Visual Studio 2015 (opens new window), Visual Studio 2017 (opens new window), Visual Studio code (opens new window) (alternative: use this extension (opens new window) for TS ❤️.2), WebStorm (opens new window) and more (opens new window)

# Installation & Usage

Please refer to the full installation & usage documentation on the TSLint website (opens new window). There, you'll find information about

  • configuration (opens new window),
  • core rules (opens new window),
  • core formatters (opens new window), and
  • customization of TSLint (opens new window).
  • inline disabling and enabling of rules with comment flags (opens new window)

# TSLint Playground

There is a sandbox environment for TSLint at palantir.github.io/tslint-playground (opens new window), which can be used to test rules and see how TSLint works. Issues can be filed against tslint-playground here (opens new window).

# Custom Rules & Plugins

# Custom rule sets from Palantir

  • tslint-react (opens new window) - Lint rules related to React & JSX.
  • tslint-blueprint (opens new window) - Lint rules to enforce best practices with blueprintjs libraries (opens new window)

# Custom rule sets from the community

If we don't have all the rules you're looking for, you can either write your own custom rules (opens new window) or use rules implementations developed by the community. The repos below are a good source of custom rules:

  • ESLint rules for TSLint (opens new window) - Improve your TSLint with the missing ESLint Rules
  • tslint-microsoft-contrib (opens new window) - A set of TSLint rules used on some Microsoft projects
  • codelyzer (opens new window) - A set of tslint rules for static code analysis of Angular TypeScript projects
  • vrsource-tslint-rules (opens new window)
  • tslint-immutable (opens new window) - TSLint rules to disable mutation in TypeScript
  • tslint-consistent-codestyle (opens new window) - TSLint rules to enforce consistent code style in TypeScript
  • tslint-sonarts (opens new window) - Bug-finding rules based on advanced code models to spot hard to find errors in TypeScript
  • tslint-clean-code (opens new window) - A set of TSLint rules inspired by the Clean Code handbook
  • rxjs-tslint-rules (opens new window) - TSLint rules for RxJS

# Development

Prerequisites:

  • node v7+
  • yarn v1.0+

# Quick Start

git clone git@github.com:palantir/tslint.git --config core.autocrlf=input --config core.eol=lf
yarn
yarn compile
yarn test
